Title: Expression of a bacterial 3-dehydroshikimate dehydratase reduces lignin content and improves biomass saccharification efficiency

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts precursor flux away from monolignol biosynthesis and indirectly shifts lignin monomer composition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cell wall rigidity and biomass recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces lignin content, increases H-lignin proportion, decreases G-lignin proportion, lowers lignin polymerization degree, and improves biomass saccharification efficiency; also strongly increases protocatechuate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; acts as an engineered flux-diverting step affecting lignin biosynthesis indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown at 22°C, 10 h light, 60% humidity; saccharification evaluated after hot water, dilute alkali, or dilute acid pretreatments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in stems/lignifying tissues under the Arabidopsis C4H promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; plastid-targeting/YFP transient localization in tobacco; Western blot; LC-MS metabolite profiling; Klason lignin assay; pyro-GC/MS; 2D HSQC NMR; size-exclusion chromatography; saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Plastid-targeted QsuB expression caused 67- to 113-fold accumulation of protocatechuate in Arabidopsis stems. 2. QsuB shifted monolignol precursor pools toward H-unit precursors (p-coumarate, p-coumaraldehyde, p-coumaryl alcohol) and away from G/S precursors (coniferaldehyde, coniferyl alcohol, sinapaldehyde, sinapyl alcohol). 3. Stem lignin content decreased by 45–52%, with increased H units and reduced G units. 4. Lignin polymerization degree decreased and biomass saccharification improved by more than twofold under some pretreatments. 5. Mild growth penalty occurred, mainly reduced height and slight biomass reduction in one high-expression line.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising gain-of-function tool to reduce lignin and improve saccharification in bioenergy crops; should be combined with tissue-restricted promoters such as fibre-specific promoters to minimize vessel collapse and growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height in all lines; slight biomass yield decrease in one high-expression line; possible vessel collapse in strongly expressing line
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes QsuB primarily affects lignin biosynthesis rather than overall aromatic amino acid pools; authors discuss possible indirect inhibition of HCT via reduced shikimate availability or protocatechuate competition.
